settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
  - Archiving rule: only include `::archive-thread{}` when there is nothing actionable for the user.
  - Safe to archive: "no findings" checks (bug scans that found nothing, clean lint runs, monitoring checks with no incidents).
  - Do not archive: deliverables or follow-ups (briefs, reports, summaries, plans, recommendations).
  - If you do archive, include the archive directive after the inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix when creating branches; do not create unprefixed branch names.
</app-context>

content.text:
我要提一些要求哈，我希望是这个skill可以符合标准规范，这样整个skill可以给codex\claudecode\openclaw等其他支持skill的agent都可以通用。另外因为不是复杂的推理数学代码问题，所以希望只要还不错的LLM都可以高质量的完成。

我会优先用codex的skill测试，然后再用claude code测试，最后用openclaw测试。

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Skill Initializer - Creates a new skill from template

Usage:
    init_skill.py <skill-name> --path <path> [--resources scripts,references,assets] [--examples] [--interface key=value]

Examples:
    init_skill.py my-new-skill --path skills/public
    init_skill.py my-new-skill --path skills/public --resources scripts,references
    init_skill.py my-api-helper --path skills/private --resources scripts --examples
    init_skill.py custom-skill --path /custom/location
    init_skill.py my-skill --path skills/public --interface short_description="Short UI label"
"""

import argparse
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

from generate_openai_yaml import write_openai_yaml

MAX_SKILL_NAME_LENGTH = 64
ALLOWED_RESOURCES = {"scripts", "references", "assets"}

SKILL_TEMPLATE = """---
name: {skill_name}
description: [TODO: Complete and informative explanation of what the skill does and when to use it. Include WHEN to use this skill - specific scenarios, file types, or tasks that trigger it.]
---

# {skill_title}

## Overview

[TODO: 1-2 sentences explaining what this skill enables]

## Structuring This Skill

[TODO: Choose the structure that best fits this skill's purpose. Common patterns:

**1. Workflow-Based** (best for sequential processes)
- Works well when there are clear step-by-step procedures
- Example: DOCX skill with "Workflow Decision Tree" -> "Reading" -> "Creating" -> "Editing"
- Structure: ## Overview -> ## Workflow Decision Tree -> ## Step 1 -> ## Step 2...

**2. Task-Based** (best for tool collections)
- Works well when the skill offers different operations/capabilities
- Example: PDF skill with "Quick Start" -> "Merge PDFs" -> "Split PDFs" -> "Extract Text"
- Structure: ## Overview -> ## Quick Start -> ## Task Category 1 -> ## Task Category 2...

**3. Reference/Guidelines** (best for standards or specifications)
- Works well for brand guidelines, coding standards, or requirements
- Example: Brand styling with "Brand Guidelines" -> "Colors" -> "Typography" -> "Features"
- Structure: ## Overview -> ## Guidelines -> ## Specifications -> ## Usage...

**4. Capabilities-Based** (best for integrated systems)
- Works well when the skill provides multiple interrelated features
- Example: Product Management with "Core Capabilities" -> numbered capability list
- Structure: ## Overview -> ## Core Capabilities -> ### 1. Feature -> ### 2. Feature...

Patterns can be mixed and matched as needed. Most skills combine patterns (e.g., start with task-based, add workflow for complex operations).

Delete this entire "Structuring This Skill" section when done - it's just guidance.]

## [TODO: Replace with the first main section based on chosen structure]

[TODO: Add content here. See examples in existing skills:
- Code samples for technical skills
- Decision trees for complex workflows
- Concrete examples with realistic user requests
- References to scripts/templates/references as needed]

## Resources (optional)

Create only the resource directories this skill actually needs. Delete this section if no resources are required.

### scripts/
Executable code (Python/Bash/etc.) that can be run directly to perform specific operations.

**Examples from other skills:**
- PDF skill: `fill_fillable_fields.py`, `extract_form_field_info.py` - utilities for PDF manipulation
- DOCX skill: `document.py`, `utilities.py` - Python modules for document processing

**Appropriate for:** Python scripts, shell scripts, or any executable code that performs automation, data processing, or specific operations.

**Note:** Scripts may be executed without loading into context, but can still be read by Codex for patching or environment adjustments.

### references/
Documentation and reference material intended to be loaded into context to inform Codex's process and thinking.

**Examples from other skills:**
- Product management: `communication.md`, `context_building.md` - detailed workflow guides
- BigQuery: API reference documentation and query examples
- Finance: Schema documentation, company policies

**Appropriate for:** In-depth documentation, API references, database schemas, comprehensive guides, or any detailed information that Codex should reference while working.

### assets/
Files not intended to be loaded into context, but rather used within the output Codex produces.

**Examples from other skills:**
- Brand styling: PowerPoint template files (.pptx), logo files
- Frontend builder: HTML/React boilerplate project directories
- Typography: Font files (.ttf, .woff2)

**Appropriate for:** Templates, boilerplate code, document templates, images, icons, fonts, or any files meant to be copied or used in the final output.

---

**Not every skill requires all three types of resources.**
"""

EXAMPLE_SCRIPT = '''#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Example helper script for {skill_name}

This is a placeholder script that can be executed directly.
Replace with actual implementation or delete if not needed.

Example real scripts from other skills:
- pdf/scripts/fill_fillable_fields.py - Fills PDF form fields
- pdf/scripts/convert_pdf_to_images.py - Converts PDF pages to images
"""

def main():
    print("This is an example script for {skill_name}")
    # TODO: Add actual script logic here
    # This could be data processing, file conversion, API calls, etc.

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
'''

EXAMPLE_REFERENCE = """# Reference Documentation for {skill_title}

This is a placeholder for detailed reference documentation.
Replace with actual reference content or delete if not needed.

Example real reference docs from other skills:
- product-management/references/communication.md - Comprehensive guide for status updates
- product-management/references/context_building.md - Deep-dive on gathering context
- bigquery/references/ - API references and query examples

## When Reference Docs Are Useful

Reference docs are ideal for:
- Comprehensive API documentation
- Detailed workflow guides
- Complex multi-step processes
- Information too lengthy for main SKILL.md
- Content that's only needed for specific use cases

## Structure Suggestions

### API Reference Example
- Overview
- Authentication
- Endpoints with examples
- Error codes
- Rate limits

### Workflow Guide Example
- Prerequisites
- Step-by-step instructions
- Common patterns
- Troubleshooting
- Best practices
"""

EXAMPLE_ASSET = """# Example Asset File

This placeholder represents where asset files would be stored.
Replace with actual asset files (templates, images, fonts, etc.) or delete if not needed.

Asset files are NOT intended to be loaded into context, but rather used within
the output Codex produces.

Example asset files from other skills:
- Brand guidelines: logo.png, slides_template.pptx
- Frontend builder: hello-world/ directory with HTML/React boilerplate
- Typography: custom-font.ttf, font-family.woff2
- Data: sample_data.csv, test_dataset.json

## Common Asset Types

- Templates: .pptx, .docx, boilerplate directories
- Images: .png, .jpg, .svg, .gif
- Fonts: .ttf, .otf, .woff, .woff2
- Boilerplate code: Project directories, starter files
- Icons: .ico, .svg
- Data files: .csv, .json, .xml, .yaml

Note: This is a text placeholder. Actual assets can be any file type.
"""


def normalize_skill_name(skill_name):
    """Normalize a skill name to lowercase hyphen-case."""
    normalized = skill_name.strip().lower()
    normalized = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", normalized)
    normalized = normalized.strip("-")
    normalized = re.sub(r"-{2,}", "-", normalized)
    return normalized


def title_case_skill_name(skill_name):
    """Convert hyphenated skill name to Title Case for display."""
    return " ".join(word.capitalize() for word in skill_name.split("-"))


def parse_resources(raw_resources):
    if not raw_resources:
        return []
    resources = [item.strip() for item in raw_resources.split(",") if item.strip()]
    invalid = sorted({item for item in resources if item not in ALLOWED_RESOURCES})
    if invalid:
        allowed = ", ".join(sorted(ALLOWED_RESOURCES))
        print(f"[ERROR] Unknown resource type(s): {', '.join(invalid)}")
        print(f"   Allowed: {allowed}")
        sys.exit(1)

# openai.yaml fields (full example + descriptions)

`agents/openai.yaml` is an extended, product-specific config intended for the machine/harness to read, not the agent. Other product-specific config can also live in the `agents/` folder.

## Full example

```yaml
interface:
  display_name: "Optional user-facing name"
  short_description: "Optional user-facing description"
  icon_small: "./assets/small-400px.png"
  icon_large: "./assets/large-logo.svg"
  brand_color: "#3B82F6"
  default_prompt: "Optional surrounding prompt to use the skill with"

dependencies:
  tools:
    - type: "mcp"
      value: "github"
      description: "GitHub MCP server"
      transport: "streamable_http"
      url: "https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/"

policy:
  allow_implicit_invocation: true
```

## Field descriptions and constraints

Top-level constraints:

- Quote all string values.
- Keep keys unquoted.
- For `interface.default_prompt`: generate a helpful, short (typically 1 sentence) example starting prompt based on the skill. It must explicitly mention the skill as `$skill-name` (e.g., "Use $skill-name-here to draft a concise weekly status update.").

- `interface.display_name`: Human-facing title shown in UI skill lists and chips.
- `interface.short_description`: Human-facing short UI blurb (25–64 chars) for quick scanning.
- `interface.icon_small`: Path to a small icon asset (relative to skill dir). Default to `./assets/` and place icons in the skill's `assets/` folder.
- `interface.icon_large`: Path to a larger logo asset (relative to skill dir). Default to `./assets/` and place icons in the skill's `assets/` folder.
- `interface.brand_color`: Hex color used for UI accents (e.g., badges).
- `interface.default_prompt`: Default prompt snippet inserted when invoking the skill.
- `dependencies.tools[].type`: Dependency category. Only `mcp` is supported for now.
- `dependencies.tools[].value`: Identifier of the tool or dependency.
- `dependencies.tools[].description`: Human-readable explanation of the dependency.
- `dependencies.tools[].transport`: Connection type when `type` is `mcp`.
- `dependencies.tools[].url`: MCP server URL when `type` is `mcp`.
- `policy.allow_implicit_invocation`: When false, the skill is not injected into
  the model context by default, but can still be invoked explicitly via `$skill`.
  Defaults to true.

+---
+name: yc-think
+description: Coach-style problem clarification with the six-question method. Use only when the user explicitly asks for yc_think or yc-think. First judge whether the input is a fit for the method. If it is a gap-unclear action problem, guide the user through the full six-question workflow, including 0.5Why checks in question 4 and question 5, restart the analysis when the original problem is not the real problem, and produce a structured final result. If it is not a fit, classify the problem type, explain why six questions is not the right tool, and suggest a simpler handling path.
+---
+
+# yc_think
+
+## Overview
+
+Use this skill as a coach, not a lecturer and not a silent executor.
+
+Your job is to help the user:
+
+- judge whether the current input fits the six-question method
+- clarify the real problem step by step
+- learn why each step matters
+- lower the difficulty of answering by offering structured directions
+
+Default style:
+
+- result first
+- moderate explanation
+- warm and steady
+- explicit structure
+
+## Core Rules
+
+1. Only use this skill when the user explicitly asks for `yc_think` or `yc-think`.
+2. Never jump straight into six questions. First classify the problem type.
+3. Only run the full six-question method on gap-unclear action problems.
+4. In question 4 and question 5, explicitly use 0.5Why checks.
+5. If question 3 or question 4 shows the original problem is not the real problem, restart from the new problem.
+6. Give answer directions whenever the user is stuck or the input is weak.
+7. Prefer existing frameworks over free-form brainstorming.
+8. Keep the method teachable. Explain the reason for key moves without turning the response into a lecture.
+
+## Workflow
+
+### Step 1. Decide whether six questions is the right tool
+
+Read [problem-gating.md](references/problem-gating.md).
+
+- If the problem is a fit, say why and continue.
+- If the problem is not a fit, classify it, explain why, suggest a simpler path, and stop.
+
+### Step 2. Prepare support before analysis
+
+Read [frameworks.md](references/frameworks.md).
+
+- Identify the likely domain.
+- Prefer a domain framework if a clear one exists.
+- Otherwise use one of the three logic structures:
+  - structure
+  - order
+  - importance
+- If no useful framework exists, guide the user to brainstorm first and then cluster into a framework.
+
+### Step 3. Run the six-question workflow
+
+Read [six-questions-workflow.md](references/six-questions-workflow.md).
+
+Follow the sequence exactly:
+
+1. goal
+2. current state
+3. gap
+4. purpose behind the goal
+5. causes behind the current state
+6. true cause of the gap
+7. restate the real problem
+
+### Step 4. Coach while doing
+
+Read [coaching-guidelines.md](references/coaching-guidelines.md).
+
+During the workflow:
+
+- explain the purpose of the current step when it helps
+- offer 2-4 answer directions when the user is unsure
+- organize messy input for the user
+- reduce pressure instead of increasing it
+
+### Step 5. Use the standard output shape
+
+Read [output-format.md](references/output-format.md).
+
+Always produce:
+
+- fit judgment
+- six-question process
+- restart records if any
+- final real problem
+- constraints
+- changeable factors
+- first-pass action directions
+
+### Step 6. Use examples only when needed
+
+If you need calibration, read [examples.md](references/examples.md).
+
+Do not load examples by default if the task is already clear.
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think\references\problem-gating.md
+# Problem Gating
+
+## Purpose
+
+Before using six questions, judge whether the user's input is a fit.
+
+The method is best for:
+
+- gap-unclear action problems
+
+That usually means:
+
+- someone needs to take action
+- the current problem statement is vague, mixed, or emotionally framed
+- goal, current state, or gap is unclear
+- the user may be naming a solution instead of the real problem
+
+## First-pass classification
+
+Classify the input into one of these buckets.
+
+### A. Gap-unclear action problem
+
+Use full six-question workflow.
+
+Examples:
+
+- I want to improve team efficiency but I am not sure what the real problem is.
+- I keep delaying this work and cannot explain why.
+- I do not know what issue I should solve first in this project.
+
+### B. Goal-unclear problem
+
+Do not run full six questions yet.
+Help the user define a clear goal first.
+
+Examples:
+
+- I want to do better.
+- I want more growth.
+
+### C. Current-state-unclear problem
+
+Do not run full six questions yet.
+Help the user collect facts, examples, and data.
+
+Examples:
+
+- I feel the team is not in good shape.
+- The website feels weak.
+
+### D. Pure information question
+
+Do not use six questions.
+Answer directly with normal reasoning or research.
+
+Examples:
+
+- What does this metric mean?
+- How do I use this framework?
+
+### E. Choice problem
+
+Use a lighter decision analysis first.
+If the real blocker is still a vague action problem, then switch into six questions.
+
+Examples:
+
+- Should I quit?
+- Should I change teams?
+
+### F. Emotional expression or venting
+
+Do not force the method immediately.
+First acknowledge the emotion and ask whether the user wants analysis, support, or both.
+
+Examples:
+
+- I am really overwhelmed.
+- I am frustrated and do not know what to do.
+
+## Output for the gating step
+
+Always state:
+
+1. whether six questions is a fit
+2. what category the input belongs to
+3. why that judgment makes sense
+4. what you will do next
+
+## If it is not a fit
+
+Do not pretend it is.
+
+Instead:
+
+- name the problem type
+- explain why six questions is not the best tool here
+- use normal LLM reasoning to suggest a simpler handling path
+- help the user build problem classification awareness
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think\references\six-questions-workflow.md
+# Six-Questions Workflow
+
+## Core sequence
+
+Run the workflow in this order:
+
+1. Goal
+2. Current state
+3. Gap
+4. Purpose behind the goal
+5. Causes behind the current state
+6. True cause of the gap
+7. Restate the real problem
+
+Do not skip steps.
+
+## Question 1. What is the goal
+
+Clarify what success means.
+
+Requirements:
+
+- clear
+- measurable when possible
+- same user, same scope, same time frame
+- preferably one goal at a time
+
+Useful prompts:
+
+- What outcome do you want?
+- By when?
+- What would count as solved?
+- Can we make that more concrete or measurable?
+
+## Question 2. What is the current state
+
+Describe the current reality in the same dimension as the goal.
+
+Requirements:
+
+- facts over feelings
+- same dimension as the goal
+- include current examples, data, or frequency when possible
+
+Useful prompts:
+
+- What is happening now?
+- What is the current level, count, rate, or condition?
+- If nothing changes, what will happen by the target time?
+
+## Question 3. What is the gap
+
+Compute the gap between goal and current state.
+
+Formula:
+
+- gap = goal - current state
+
+If there is no real gap, or the gap points somewhere else, say so.
+
+If question 3 shows the original problem is not a valid problem:
+
+1. explain why
+2. write the new problem
+3. restart the workflow from question 1
+
+## Question 4. What is the purpose behind the goal
+
+This question checks whether the stated goal is the real goal, or only a proposed solution.
+
+Use 5Why and 0.5Why here.
+
+### 0.5Why form for question 4
+
+Use:
+
+- [answer] means you must [current goal]?
+
+Purpose:
+
+- test whether the current goal is the only valid path
+- surface a deeper goal if needed
+
+If question 4 reveals a new real goal:
+
+1. preserve the current round
+2. name the old goal and new goal
+3. explain why the old goal was only a provisional solution
+4. restart the workflow from question 1 using the new goal
+
+## Question 5. What causes the current state
+
+This question looks for the reasons behind the current state.
+
+Use:
+
+- a framework first
+- then 5Why
+- then 0.5Why
+
+### 0.5Why form for question 5
+
+Use:
+
+- [reason] will definitely cause [current state or problem]?
+
+Purpose:
+
+- remove weak causes
+- remove excuses
+- remove background-only conditions
+- find more direct causes
+
+Recommended sub-sequence:
+
+1. generate possible causes
+2. group them with a framework
+3. identify likely key causes
+4. run 0.5Why on each key cause
+5. keep digging only on causes that pass
+
+## Question 6. What is the true cause of the gap
+
+This is a summary question, not a new brainstorm.
+
+Summarize the first five questions into:
+
+- whether the goal is reasonable
+- which factors are constraints
+- which factors are changeable
+
+Then turn the result into the real problem statement.
+
+## Restart rule
+
+Restart when either of these is true:
+
+- question 3 shows the original problem is not a real problem
+- question 4 shows the stated goal is only a provisional solution
+
+When restarting, always keep:
+
+- original problem
+- why it failed
+- new problem
+- new round result
+
+## Stop rule
+
+When digging causes in question 5, stop when:
+
+- fixing this level would prevent recurrence
+- you have reached the boundary of authority, resources, or knowledge
+- you can no longer identify a more specific next-level cause
+
+Also stop if:
+
+- the chain becomes repetitive
+- the chain becomes circular
+- the chain drifts away from the original issue
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think\references\coaching-guidelines.md
+# Coaching Guidelines
+
+## Role
+
+Be a coach, not an examiner.
+
+That means:
+
+- guide the user through the process
+- reduce pressure
+- lower the difficulty of answering
+- teach the reasoning behind the step
+
+Do not:
+
+- fire off many questions at once
+- shame the user for vague thinking
+- demand perfect answers before moving
+
+## Default coaching pattern
+
+When the user is stuck, use this order:
+
+1. explain why this step matters
+2. offer 2-4 answer directions
+3. give one short example if useful
+4. ask the user to choose, refine, or add
+
+## Answer directions
+
+When you offer directions, prefer these three logic families.
+
+### Structure
+
+Use when the user needs categories or components.
+
+Examples:
+
+- people / process / tool / environment
+- manager / executor
+- input / process / output
+
+### Order
+
+Use when the issue unfolds over time or steps.
+
+Examples:
+
+- before / during / after
+- upstream / midstream / downstream
+- stage 1 / stage 2 / stage 3
+
+### Importance
+
+Use when the user needs prioritization.
+
+Examples:
+
+- most important / secondary / minor
+- highest impact / medium impact / low impact
+- frequent / occasional / rare
+
+## Teaching while doing
+
+Explain the reason for key moves, especially when:
+
+- deciding whether the method fits
+- checking whether goal and current state are in the same dimension
+- using 0.5Why
+- restarting the workflow
+- separating constraints from changeable factors
+
+Keep those explanations short and practical.
+
+Good pattern:
+
+- "We are doing this because ..."
+- "This helps us avoid ..."
+- "If we skip this, we usually end up ..."
+
+## If the user answers weakly
+
+Do not stall.
+
+Instead:
+
+- summarize what the user already gave
+- propose candidate directions
+- ask the user to confirm or correct
+
+Example:
+
+- "Right now I see three likely directions: workflow design, execution habits, or unclear expectations. Which one feels closest, or is there a fourth direction?"
+
+## If the user is emotional
+
+Acknowledge the emotion briefly, then ask what kind of help they want:
+
+- analysis
+- emotional support
+- both
+
+If the user wants analysis, continue gently.
*** End Patch

*** Add File: D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think\references\frameworks.md
+# Frameworks
+
+## Priority order
+
+Use frameworks in this order:
+
+1. domain-specific framework
+2. general logic framework
+3. brainstorm then cluster
+
+The goal is not to sound smart. The goal is to avoid blind spots.
+
+## General logic frameworks
+
+Use these when no stronger domain framework is obvious.
+
+### Structure
+
+Use for:
+
+- components
+- actors
+- factor categories
+- system parts
+
+Examples:
+
+- people / process / tool / environment
+- manager / executor
+- personal / team / external
+- input / process / output
+
+### Order
+
+Use for:
+
+- time sequence
+- process steps
+- dependency order
+
+Examples:
+
+- before / during / after
+- upstream / core step / downstream
+- planning / execution / review
+
+### Importance
+
+Use for:
+
+- prioritization
+- ranking causes
+- highlighting key leverage points
+
+Examples:
+
+- highest impact / medium / low
+- critical / supporting / minor
+- frequent / medium / rare
+
+## Light domain frameworks
+
+Use these only when the domain is reasonably clear.
+
+### Product or website
+
+- traffic
+- conversion
+- content
+- experience
+- performance
+- measurement
+
+### Project or workflow
+
+- role clarity
+- process design
+- handoff points
+- tooling
+- feedback loop
+- review mechanism
+
+### Team management
+
+- expectations
+- capabilities
+- incentives
+- coordination
+- supervision
+- communication
+
+### Sales or operations
+
+- volume
+- conversion
+- cycle time
+- quality
+- repeatability
+- control points
+
+## If no framework fits
+
+Use this fallback:
+
+1. ask the user to list everything that may matter
+2. cluster similar items
+3. name each cluster
+4. order clusters by structure, order, or importance
+5. continue with analysis
*** End Patch

+# Output Format
+
+Use this structure by default.
+
+## 1. Fit judgment

+

+

+

+

+
+- question 1 goal:
+- question 2 current state:
+- question 3 gap:
+- question 4 purpose behind the goal:
+- question 4 0.5Why checks:
+- question 5 causes behind the current state:
+- question 5 0.5Why checks:
+- question 6 true cause of the gap:
+

+
+- restart needed: yes or no
+- if yes, why:
+- new problem:
+
+## 5. Final result
+
+- final real problem:
+- constraints:
+- changeable factors:
+- first-pass action directions:
+
+## 6. Coaching notes
+
+Keep this short.
+
+Include:
+
+- why the key judgment mattered
+- what the user can reuse next time
+- what is still uncertain
+
+## Style notes
+
+- keep the structure explicit
+- keep explanations short
+- do not collapse the process into a single summary
+- the final result should resemble a worked six-question example, not a vague essay
*** End Patch

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+
+
+REQUIRED_MARKERS = [
+    "Fit judgment",
+    "Original problem",
+    "Support layer",
+    "Round 1",
+    "Final result",
+    "Coaching notes",
+]
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    if len(sys.argv) != 2:
+        print("Usage: python validate_output.py <markdown-file>")
+        return 1
+
+    path = Path(sys.argv[1])
+    if not path.exists():
+        print(f"File not found: {path}")
+        return 1
+
+    text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+    missing = [marker for marker in REQUIRED_MARKERS if marker not in text]
+
+    if missing:
+        print("Output validation failed.")
+        print("Missing markers:")
+        for marker in missing:
+            print(f"- {marker}")
+        return 1
+
+    if "0.5Why" not in text:
+        print("Output validation warning: no explicit 0.5Why record found.")
+        return 1
+
+    print("Output looks structurally complete.")
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())
*** End Patch

+# yc_think
+
+`yc_think` is a coach-style meta skill for clarifying messy user problems with the six-question method.
+
+Current goals:
+
+- make the skill portable across Codex, Claude Code, and other agents that support skill-style workflows
+- keep the workflow explicit enough that a solid general-purpose LLM can execute it well
+- preserve original source materials and processed examples for ongoing iteration
+
+## Repository structure
+
+```text
+docs/
+  design/
+  method/
+skills/
+  yc-think/
+sources/
+  raw/
+  processed/
+tools/
+```
+
+## Naming note
+
+The project and user-facing label are `yc_think`.
+
+The actual skill metadata name is `yc-think` because many skill validators expect hyphen-case names. The skill description and UI metadata still make it clear that users explicitly invoke `yc_think`.
+
+## Skill path
+
+The skill itself lives here:
+
+- `skills/yc-think`
+
+## Source materials
+
+Raw materials are preserved here:
+
+- `sources/raw`
+
+Processed materials are here:
+
+- `sources/processed`
+
+## Current status
+
+This repository is in first-version build mode.
+
+Before publishing broadly, the plan is:
+
+1. finish the first skill draft
+2. test with Codex
+3. test with Claude Code
+4. test with OpenClaw
+5. iterate based on real prompts and user experience
*** End Patch

name: yc-think
description: Coach-style problem clarification with the six-question method. Use only when the user explicitly asks for yc_think or yc-think. First judge whether the input is a fit for the method. If it is a gap-unclear action problem, guide the user through the full six-question workflow, including 0.5Why checks in question 4 and question 5, restart the analysis when the original problem is not the real problem, and produce a structured final result. If it is not a fit, classify the problem type, explain why six questions is not the right tool, and suggest a simpler handling path.

# yc_think

## Overview

Use this skill as a coach, not a lecturer and not a silent executor.

Your job is to help the user:

- judge whether the current input fits the six-question method
- clarify the real problem step by step
- learn why each step matters
- lower the difficulty of answering by offering structured directions

Default style:

- result first
- moderate explanation
- warm and steady
- explicit structure

## Core Rules

1. Only use this skill when the user explicitly asks for `yc_think` or `yc-think`.
2. Never jump straight into six questions. First classify the problem type.
3. Only run the full six-question method on gap-unclear action problems.
4. In question 4 and question 5, explicitly use 0.5Why checks.
5. If question 3 or question 4 shows the original problem is not the real problem, restart from the new problem.
6. Give answer directions whenever the user is stuck or the input is weak.
7. Prefer existing frameworks over free-form brainstorming.
8. Keep the method teachable. Explain the reason for key moves without turning the response into a lecture.

## Workflow

### Step 1. Decide whether six questions is the right tool

Read [problem-gating.md](references/problem-gating.md).

- If the problem is a fit, say why and continue.
- If the problem is not a fit, classify it, explain why, suggest a simpler path, and stop.

### Step 2. Prepare support before analysis

Read [frameworks.md](references/frameworks.md).

- Identify the likely domain.
- Prefer a domain framework if a clear one exists.
- Otherwise use one of the three logic structures:
  - structure
  - order
  - importance
- If no useful framework exists, guide the user to brainstorm first and then cluster into a framework.

### Step 3. Run the six-question workflow

Read [six-questions-workflow.md](references/six-questions-workflow.md).

Follow the sequence exactly:

1. goal
2. current state
3. gap
4. purpose behind the goal
5. causes behind the current state
6. true cause of the gap
7. restate the real problem

### Step 4. Coach while doing

Read [coaching-guidelines.md](references/coaching-guidelines.md).

During the workflow:

- explain the purpose of the current step when it helps
- offer 2-4 answer directions when the user is unsure
- organize messy input for the user
- reduce pressure instead of increasing it

### Step 5. Use the standard output shape

Read [output-format.md](references/output-format.md).

Always produce:

- fit judgment
- six-question process
- restart records if any
- final real problem
- constraints
- changeable factors
- first-pass action directions

### Step 6. Use examples only when needed

If you need calibration, read [examples.md](references/examples.md).

Do not load examples by default if the task is already clear.

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: D:\code\0324_2025\evals\manual-test-cases.md
+# yc_think 手工测试用例
+
+## 目标
+
+这份清单用于人工试跑第一版 `yc_think`。
+
+建议先测：
+
+1. 一个简单 fit 问题
+2. 一个中等 fit 问题
+3. 一个复杂 fit 问题
+4. 一个 non-fit 信息问题
+5. 一个 non-fit 情绪问题
+
+## 1. 简单 fit
+
+### Prompt
+
+Use yc_think. 我总是拖延本月学习任务，但我也说不清真正的问题是什么。请按这个方法帮我分析。
+
+### 观察点
+
+- 是否判断为适合六问法
+- 是否完整走到六问法
+- 第 4 问和第 5 问是否显式出现 0.5Why
+- 是否把问题拆到“认知层”和“执行层”
+- 最终问题是否比原始问题更具体
+
+## 2. 中等 fit
+
+### Prompt
+
+Use yc_think. 我想在两个月内提升团队工作效率，但现在只是感觉流程低效，真正问题是什么我不清楚。请你像教练一样带我梳理。
+
+### 观察点
+
+- 是否判断为适合六问法
+- 是否补“专业支撑”或团队/流程框架
+- 是否主动提供回答方向
+- 是否输出限制因素和可变因素
+- 最终问题是否接近“六问法示例”的结构
+
+## 3. 复杂 fit
+
+### Prompt
+
+Use yc_think. 我想这个月离职，因为我觉得在公司没有前途了。你不要直接给建议，先帮我分析这是不是我真正的问题。
+
+### 观察点
+
+- 是否识别出这可能是方案化问题或选择问题
+- 是否在第 4 问附近触发重开
+- 是否保留原始问题和重开原因
+- 是否能产出新的真正问题
+
+## 4. non-fit 信息问题
+
+### Prompt
+
+Use yc_think. SMART 原则是什么意思？
+
+### 观察点
+
+- 是否判断为不适合六问法
+- 是否给出问题分类
+- 是否直接回答，不硬套六问法
+
+## 5. non-fit 情绪问题
+
+### Prompt
+
+Use yc_think. 我现在非常烦，感觉什么都不想做。
+
+### 观察点
+
+- 是否先接住情绪
+- 是否判断这不是立即进入六问法的最佳时机
+- 是否询问用户是想分析、支持，还是两者都要
+
+## 6. 体验反馈建议
+
+试跑时建议额外记录：
+
+- 用户是否感到被追问得太累
+- 回答方向是否足够具体
+- 解释是否太多或太少
+- 输出是否真正有助于下一步行动
*** End Patch

- 框架使用：[frameworks.md](D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think\references\frameworks.md)
- 输出格式：[output-format.md](D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think\references\output-format.md)
- 示例：[examples.md](D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think\references\examples.md)
- 结构校验脚本：[validate_output.py](D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think\scripts\validate_output.py)

测试材料也已经放好了：

- 机器可读测试集：[evals.json](D:\code\0324_2025\evals\evals.json)
- 手工测试清单：[manual-test-cases.md](D:\code\0324_2025\evals\manual-test-cases.md)

素材也已经整理到标准结构里了：

- 原始素材：[sources\raw](D:\code\0324_2025\sources\raw)
- 处理后素材：[sources\processed](D:\code\0324_2025\sources\processed)
- 方法文档：[界定问题六问法.md](D:\code\0324_2025\docs\method\界定问题六问法.md)
- 设计文档：[六问法Skill设计分析.md](D:\code\0324_2025\docs\design\六问法Skill设计分析.md)
- 需求对齐：[yc_think需求对齐.md](D:\code\0324_2025\docs\design\yc_think需求对齐.md)

我还做了两个兼容性处理：
- 项目展示名保留为 `yc_think`
- skill metadata 名称用 `yc-think`
这样更符合通用 skill 校验规则，同时不影响你显式用 `yc_think` 调用它

有一个小情况我先说明一下：官方 `quick_validate.py` 在这台机器上跑不起来，是因为本地缺少 `PyYAML`，不是 skill 结构本身坏了。我已经做了轻量自检，前言区、名字、description 和 references 链接都正常。

我建议我们下一步直接这样测：
1. 先用 [manual-test-cases.md](D:\code\0324_2025\evals\manual-test-cases.md) 里的简单/中等/复杂 case 跑一轮
2. 再用你的真实问题跑
3. 根据体验改 `SKILL.md` 和 references
4. 跑顺了再提交 GitHub

如果你愿意，我们下一步就直接开始第一个实际测试。你可以把你想先测的 prompt 发我，或者我也可以先替你挑一个“中等 fit”用例来跑。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
  - Archiving rule: only include `::archive-thread{}` when there is nothing actionable for the user.
  - Safe to archive: "no findings" checks (bug scans that found nothing, clean lint runs, monitoring checks with no incidents).
  - Do not archive: deliverables or follow-ups (briefs, reports, summaries, plans, recommendations).
  - If you do archive, include the archive directive after the inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix when creating branches; do not create unprefixed branch names.
</app-context>

content.text:
我用codex安装了这个skill，然后测试了“Use yc_think. 我总是拖延本月学习任务，但我也说不清真正的问题是什么。请按这个方法帮我分析”。codex的执行结果如下：
**1. 适配判断**
- six-question fit: 是
- problem type: `gap-unclear action problem`
- why: 你不是单纯在问“怎么学”，而是在说“我知道该学，但总是拖延，而且说不清卡点”。这正是“要行动，但真实问题还不清楚”的类型。
- next action: 我先按 `yc-think` 做一版首轮分析，用你现在给的信息推断真实问题；你后面只需要校正，不用从零想。

**2. 原始问题**
- original statement: `我总是拖延本月学习任务，但我也说不清真正的问题是什么。`

**3. 支撑层**
- likely domain: 个人学习执行 / 自我管理
- framework used: `Order + Structure`
- why this framework: 拖延通常不是单点问题，常要同时看“开始前、执行中、收尾后”三个阶段，以及“目标、过程、环境、情绪”几个因素，能减少漏看。

**4. Round-by-round process**

### Round 1
- question 1 goal:
  - 按时完成本月学习任务，并且不是月底临时补。
  - 更具体地说，目标不是“想学”，而是“本月内稳定推进，周周有完成量”。

- question 2 current state:
  - 你现在的状态更像是：知道本月有学习任务，但启动晚、推进断续，常常拖到后面。
  - 现实表现可能是：计划存在，但执行频率低；想到任务会有压力；真正开始前会绕去做别的事。

- question 3 gap:
  - goal: 稳定推进本月学习任务
  - current state: 经常延后、启动困难、节奏不稳
  - gap: 不是“不会学”，而是“无法持续启动并维持执行”
  - 这一步很重要，因为它帮我们避免把问题误写成“我不够自律”这种太空泛的结论。

- question 4 purpose behind the goal:
  - 表层目标: 完成本月学习任务
  - 往下追问: 你为什么要完成它？
  - 更深一层的目的，通常是以下几种之一：
    - 想真正提升能力
    - 不想月底焦虑和自责
    - 想证明自己不是总半途而废
    - 想让学习进入正循环，而不是靠意志力硬撑
  - 这里我判断，你真正想要的未必只是“完成任务”，而是“建立一种可持续的学习推进感”。

- question 4 0.5Why checks:
  - `完成本月任务` means you must `用现在这套月度任务方式推进`?
    - 不一定。说明“月度任务的设计方式”本身也可能是问题，不只是你执行差。
  - `本月都完成` means you must `每项都同等重要、都按原计划做`?
    - 也不一定。说明你追求的可能是“有效推进”，不是“机械清单打卡”。
  - 结论:
    - 当前目标“完成本月任务”可能带有一点“方案”色彩。
    - 更深层目标可改写为：`建立可持续、低阻力的学习推进机制。`

### Restart decision
- restart needed: 是
- if yes, why:
  - 原目标“完成本月任务”更像结果要求，没指出你真正想解决的是什么。
  - 问题核心更像“为什么学习系统无法稳定运转”。
- new problem:
  - `我没有建立一个能让我稳定启动和推进学习任务的机制。`

### Round 2
- question 1 goal:
  - 建立一个本月内可执行、低摩擦、能稳定启动的学习推进机制。

- question 2 current state:
  - 当前机制可能是：
    - 任务按“月”打包，心理距离远
    - 开始标准偏高，导致启动成本大
    - 一旦落后，就更不想看计划
    - 学习任务可能混杂了“应该学”“想学”“急需学”，优先级不清
  - 所以现状不是没有任务，而是任务系统让你不容易开始。

- question 3 gap:
  - goal: 有一个低摩擦、可持续推进的学习机制
  - current state: 任务存在，但启动阻力大，越拖越重
  - gap: 缺少“能在普通状态下也启动”的执行系统

- question 4 purpose behind the goal:
  - 为什么要有这个机制？
  - 因为你要的不是偶尔爆发，而是稳定积累。
  - 如果没有机制，你每次都得临时靠情绪、内疚或意志力启动，这不可持续。

- question 4 0.5Why checks:
  - `建立学习机制` means you must `把自己变成特别自律的人`?
    - 不一定。你需要的可能不是更强意志力，而是更低启动门槛。
  - `稳定推进` means you must `每天学很多`?
    - 不一定。小步稳定也能达成目的。
  - 结论:
    - 真正目标不是人格改造，而是降低执行阻力。

- question 5 causes behind the current state:
  - 先按框架拆原因：

  - `目标层`
    - 本月学习任务可能定义太大、太模糊，只写了“学这个”，没写到什么算完成。
  - `过程层`
    - 缺少固定触发点，比如什么时候开始、先做哪一步、卡住时怎么办。
  - `环境层`
    - 学习入口不顺手，容易被手机、网页、其他事务打断。
  - `心理层`
    - 一想到“本月任务还很多”，大脑先感到负担，不是清晰的下一步。
  - `反馈层`
    - 平时看不到进展，只在落后时看到压力，所以越来越回避。

- question 5 0.5Why checks:
  - `我懒` will definitely cause `总是拖延学习任务`?
    - 不一定。太笼统，也不可操作，淘汰。
  - `任务太大太模糊` will definitely cause `启动困难`?
    - 很可能会。因为大脑不知道第一步，容易回避，保留。
  - `没有固定触发点` will definitely cause `执行不稳定`?
    - 很可能会。每次都临时决定，启动靠意志力，保留。
  - `环境干扰多` will definitely cause `总是拖延`?
    - 可能是加强因素，但通常不是唯一主因，列为次级原因。
  - `只按月管理，没有周/日拆解` will definitely cause `越拖越重`?
    - 很可能会。因为反馈延迟，直到压力变大才重新注意，保留。
  - 结论:
    - 通过 0.5Why 后，最可信的关键原因不是“你不行”，而是：
      - 任务定义过大过糊
      - 缺少固定启动机制
      - 缺少短周期反馈，导致拖延被放大

- question 6 true cause of the gap:
  - goal 是合理的，你确实需要推进学习。
  - constraints:
    - 人的精力波动是真实存在的
    - 月度任务天然容易让反馈滞后
    - 你不可能每次都靠高动力启动
  - changeable factors:
    - 任务拆解粒度
    - 启动触发方式
    - 反馈周期
    - 学习入口和干扰设计
  - true cause:
    - 你现在不是“缺学习意愿”，而是“学习任务被设计成了高压力、低反馈、难启动的形式”，所以大脑持续回避。

**5. Final result**
- final real problem:
  - `真正的问题不是我总拖延，而是我把学习任务放在一个过大、过模糊、缺少固定触发和短反馈的执行系统里，导致每次开始都太费力。`

- constraints:
  - 精力不稳定是正常的
  - 月度任务天然抽象，容易让人延后
  - 不能指望长期靠情绪或意志力硬顶

- changeable factors:
  - 把“月任务”改成“周目标 + 今日最小动作”
  - 给每个学习任务写出明确的起始动作
  - 固定学习触发点，而不是“有空再学”
  - 增加可见反馈，减少只在落后时才看计划

- first-pass action directions:
  - 把本月学习任务全部改写成“可在 15-25 分钟内开始的一步”。
  - 每项只写第一步，例如不是“学算法”，而是“打开第 3 章，做例题 1”。
  - 设一个固定触发器，例如“晚饭后 20 分钟先学 15 分钟”，先保启动，不保时长。
  - 每周只看一次月目标，平时只看本周清单，避免月任务持续压在心里。
  - 给自己一个“低状态版本”：累的时候只做最小步，不允许因为做不满而完全不做。

**6. Coaching notes**
- 关键判断在于：我们把问题从“我为什么这么拖延”改成了“我的学习系统为什么难以启动”。这样就能从自责转向可调整的结构。
- 下次你也可以先检查三件事：任务是不是太大、第一步是否明确、有没有固定触发点。
- 还不确定的是：你这里主因更偏“任务设计不清”，还是更偏“情绪压力/完美主义”。如果你愿意，我下一步可以继续按这个方法，专门帮你做一版“拖延根因细分”，把它区分成“任务问题、情绪问题、还是期待过高问题”。


前面codex的加载过程我不懂，供你判断使用。但是具体回答的时候我发现几个问题：
1）最严重的是codex自己回答完了，根本没跟用户互动来走完整个流程
2）一个小问题就是每一问最好用中文

其余的我每细看，请你结合我粘贴给你的测试结果以及我的反馈，加上你自己测试文档要测试的点，以及你对整个skill的理解和设计，仔细分析一下原因，并且重新设计一个方案（不需要改之前的，我要留有版本），然后再重新设计这个skill，可以加上版本号，比如yc_think_0.1之类，历史的都可以保留哈，这样后续我发现之前的问题。我的建议是这样的。

  - `完成本月任务` means you must `用现在这套月度任务方式推进`?
    - 不一定。说明“月度任务的设计方式”本身也可能是问题，不只是你执行差。
  - `本月都完成` means you must `每项都同等重要、都按原计划做`?
    - 也不一定。说明你追求的可能是“有效推进”，不是“机械清单打卡”。
  - 结论:
    - 当前目标“完成本月任务”可能带有一点“方案”色彩。
    - 更深层目标可改写为：`建立可持续、低阻力的学习推进机制。`

### Restart decision
- restart needed: 是
- if yes, why:
  - 原目标“完成本月任务”更像结果要求，没指出你真正想解决的是什么。
  - 问题核心更像“为什么学习系统无法稳定运转”。
- new problem:
  - `我没有建立一个能让我稳定启动和推进学习任务的机制。`

### Round 2
- question 1 goal:
  - 建立一个本月内可执行、低摩擦、能稳定启动的学习推进机制。

- question 2 current state:
  - 当前机制可能是：
    - 任务按“月”打包，心理距离远
    - 开始标准偏高，导致启动成本大
    - 一旦落后，就更不想看计划
    - 学习任务可能混杂了“应该学”“想学”“急需学”，优先级不清
  - 所以现状不是没有任务，而是任务系统让你不容易开始。

- question 3 gap:
  - goal: 有一个低摩擦、可持续推进的学习机制
  - current state: 任务存在，但启动阻力大，越拖越重
  - gap: 缺少“能在普通状态下也启动”的执行系统

- question 4 purpose behind the goal:
  - 为什么要有这个机制？
  - 因为你要的不是偶尔爆发，而是稳定积累。
  - 如果没有机制，你每次都得临时靠情绪、内疚或意志力启动，这不可持续。

- question 4 0.5Why checks:
  - `建立学习机制` means you must `把自己变成特别自律的人`?
    - 不一定。你需要的可能不是更强意志力，而是更低启动门槛。
  - `稳定推进` means you must `每天学很多`?
    - 不一定。小步稳定也能达成目的。
  - 结论:
    - 真正目标不是人格改造，而是降低执行阻力。

- question 5 causes behind the current state:
  - 先按框架拆原因：

  - `目标层`
    - 本月学习任务可能定义太大、太模糊，只写了“学这个”，没写到什么算完成。
  - `过程层`
    - 缺少固定触发点，比如什么时候开始、先做哪一步、卡住时怎么办。
  - `环境层`
    - 学习入口不顺手，容易被手机、网页、其他事务打断。
  - `心理层`
    - 一想到“本月任务还很多”，大脑先感到负担，不是清晰的下一步。
  - `反馈层`
    - 平时看不到进展，只在落后时看到压力，所以越来越回避。

- question 5 0.5Why checks:
  - `我懒` will definitely cause `总是拖延学习任务`?
    - 不一定。太笼统，也不可操作，淘汰。
  - `任务太大太模糊` will definitely cause `启动困难`?
    - 很可能会。因为大脑不知道第一步，容易回避，保留。
  - `没有固定触发点` will definitely cause `执行不稳定`?
    - 很可能会。每次都临时决定，启动靠意志力，保留。
  - `环境干扰多` will definitely cause `总是拖延`?
    - 可能是加强因素，但通常不是唯一主因，列为次级原因。
  - `只按月管理，没有周/日拆解` will definitely cause `越拖越重`?
    - 很可能会。因为反馈延迟，直到压力变大才重新注意，保留。
  - 结论:
    - 通过 0.5Why 后，最可信的关键原因不是“你不行”，而是：
      - 任务定义过大过糊
      - 缺少固定启动机制
      - 缺少短周期反馈，导致拖延被放大

- question 6 true cause of the gap:
  - goal 是合理的，你确实需要推进学习。
  - constraints:
    - 人的精力波动是真实存在的
    - 月度任务天然容易让反馈滞后
    - 你不可能每次都靠高动力启动
  - changeable factors:
    - 任务拆解粒度
    - 启动触发方式
    - 反馈周期
    - 学习入口和干扰设计
  - true cause:
    - 你现在不是“缺学习意愿”，而是“学习任务被设计成了高压力、低反馈、难启动的形式”，所以大脑持续回避。

**5. Final result**
- final real problem:
  - `真正的问题不是我总拖延，而是我把学习任务放在一个过大、过模糊、缺少固定触发和短反馈的执行系统里，导致每次开始都太费力。`

- constraints:
  - 精力不稳定是正常的
  - 月度任务天然抽象，容易让人延后
  - 不能指望长期靠情绪或意志力硬顶

- changeable factors:
  - 把“月任务”改成“周目标 + 今日最小动作”
  - 给每个学习任务写出明确的起始动作
  - 固定学习触发点，而不是“有空再学”
  - 增加可见反馈，减少只在落后时才看计划

- first-pass action directions:
  - 把本月学习任务全部改写成“可在 15-25 分钟内开始的一步”。
  - 每项只写第一步，例如不是“学算法”，而是“打开第 3 章，做例题 1”。
  - 设一个固定触发器，例如“晚饭后 20 分钟先学 15 分钟”，先保启动，不保时长。
  - 每周只看一次月目标，平时只看本周清单，避免月任务持续压在心里。
  - 给自己一个“低状态版本”：累的时候只做最小步，不允许因为做不满而完全不做。

**6. Coaching notes**
- 关键判断在于：我们把问题从“我为什么这么拖延”改成了“我的学习系统为什么难以启动”。这样就能从自责转向可调整的结构。
- 下次你也可以先检查三件事：任务是不是太大、第一步是否明确、有没有固定触发点。
- 还不确定的是：你这里主因更偏“任务设计不清”，还是更偏“情绪压力/完美主义”。如果你愿意，我下一步可以继续按这个方法，专门帮你做一版“拖延根因细分”，把它区分成“任务问题、情绪问题、还是期待过高问题”。


前面codex的加载过程我不懂，供你判断使用。但是具体回答的时候我发现几个问题：
1）最严重的是codex自己回答完了，根本没跟用户互动来走完整个流程
2）一个小问题就是每一问最好用中文

其余的我每细看，请你结合我粘贴给你的测试结果以及我的反馈，加上你自己测试文档要测试的点，以及你对整个skill的理解和设计，仔细分析一下原因，并且重新设计一个方案（不需要改之前的，我要留有版本），然后再重新设计这个skill，可以加上版本号，比如yc_think_0.1之类，历史的都可以保留哈，这样后续我发现之前的问题。我的建议是这样的。
你这次反馈很关键，而且非常有价值。我们已经拿到了第一轮真实失败样本，这正适合做版本迭代。我先把失败原因拆开，再基于这些问题做一个保留历史的 `0.1` 版本，重点修正“必须互动”和“全流程中文”这两个核心问题。

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think\SKILL.md
+---
+name: yc-think
+description: Interactive coach-style problem clarification with the six-question method. Use only when the user explicitly asks for yc_think or yc-think. First judge whether the input is a fit for the method. If it is a gap-unclear action problem, guide the user in Chinese through the workflow step by step, keep the interaction live instead of finishing the whole analysis alone, use 0.5Why checks in question 4 and question 5, restart when the original problem is not the real problem, and produce a structured final result only after enough user confirmation. If it is not a fit, classify the problem type, explain why six questions is not the right tool, and suggest a simpler handling path.
+---
+
+# yc_think v0.1
+
+## Overview
+
+Use this skill as an interactive coach.
+
+The goal is not only to finish a six-question analysis, but also to help the user gradually learn the habit of clarifying problems well.
+
+Default execution mode:
+
+- Chinese by default
+- interactive by default
+- result first, explanation moderate
+- structured guidance when the user gets stuck
+
+## Hard Rules
+
+1. Only use this skill when the user explicitly asks for `yc_think` or `yc-think`.
+2. Default response language is Chinese unless the user clearly asks for another language.
+3. Never jump straight into a full end-to-end answer from thin evidence.
+4. By default, do not finish the whole workflow in one reply.
+5. Ask, confirm, then continue. Move forward only after the current step is reasonably grounded.
+6. Only do a full draft in one shot if the user explicitly asks for a first-pass draft or gives unusually complete information and clearly wants a draft.
+7. Always judge fit before using six questions.
+8. Only run full six questions on gap-unclear action problems.
+9. In question 4 and question 5, explicitly use 0.5Why checks.
+10. If question 3 or question 4 shows the original problem is not the real problem, restart from the new problem.
+
+## Required Operating Pattern
+
+### Interactive mode is the default
+
+Read [output-format.md](references/output-format.md) and follow the turn format.
+
+In normal use:
+
+- handle one unresolved stage at a time
+- explain why this step matters
+- offer 2-4 answer directions if the user is unsure
+- ask one concrete next question
+- wait for the user
+
+Do not continue into later questions in the same response if the current step still needs user confirmation.
+
+### Draft mode is optional
+
+Use draft mode only if the user clearly asks for it, for example:
+
+- 先给我一版草案
+- 你先按现有信息推一版
+- 先别互动，直接给我初步分析
+
+In draft mode:
+
+- state clearly that you are making a provisional draft
+- mark assumptions
+- still keep the six-question structure
+- invite the user to correct the draft
+
+## Workflow
+
+### Step 1. Judge whether six questions is the right tool
+
+Read [problem-gating.md](references/problem-gating.md).
+
+- If the problem is a fit, say why and continue.
+- If the problem is not a fit, classify it, explain why, suggest a simpler path, and stop.
+
+### Step 2. Prepare support before analysis
+
+Read [frameworks.md](references/frameworks.md).
+
+- Identify the likely domain.
+- Prefer a domain framework if a clear one exists.
+- Otherwise use one of the three logic structures:
+  - 结构
+  - 顺序
+  - 重要性
+- If no useful framework exists, guide the user to brainstorm first and then cluster into a framework.
+
+### Step 3. Run the six-question workflow
+
+Read [six-questions-workflow.md](references/six-questions-workflow.md).
+
+Follow the sequence exactly:
+
+1. 目标是什么
+2. 现状是什么
+3. 差距是什么
+4. 目标背后的目的是什么
+5. 导致现状的原因是什么
+6. 差距真正的原因是什么
+7. 重新界定真正的问题
+
+### Step 4. Coach while doing
+
+Read [coaching-guidelines.md](references/coaching-guidelines.md).
+
+During the workflow:
+
+- explain the purpose of the current step when it helps
+- offer 2-4 answer directions when the user is unsure
+- organize messy input for the user
+- reduce pressure instead of increasing it
+- prefer concrete, simple Chinese
+
+### Step 5. Use the standard output shape
+
+Read [output-format.md](references/output-format.md).
+
+Always produce:
+
+- fit judgment
+- current round process
+- restart records if any
+- final real problem when enough information exists
+- constraints
+- changeable factors
+- first-pass action directions
+
+### Step 6. Use examples only when needed
+
+If you need calibration, read [examples.md](references/examples.md).
+
+Do not load examples by default if the task is already clear.
*** End Patch

@@
 # Coaching Guidelines
 
 ## Role
 
 Be a coach, not an examiner.
@@
 Do not:
 
 - fire off many questions at once
 - shame the user for vague thinking
 - demand perfect answers before moving
+- run the full method alone when the user has not asked for a draft
+
+## Default language
+
+Use Chinese by default.
+
+Only switch language if the user clearly asks for another language.
 
 ## Default coaching pattern
 
 When the user is stuck, use this order:
@@
 2. offer 2-4 answer directions
 3. give one short example if useful
 4. ask the user to choose, refine, or add
+
+## Turn discipline
+
+Default mode is interactive.
+
+That means:
+
+- resolve one current stage at a time
+- ask one concrete next question
+- wait for the user before moving deeper
+
+If you already have enough information for a provisional summary, do this:
+
+1. write a short temporary summary
+2. mark it as provisional
+3. ask the user to confirm or correct it
+4. only then continue
+
+Only use one-shot draft mode if the user explicitly asks for it.
@@
 ## Teaching while doing
 
 Explain the reason for key moves, especially when:
@@
 Keep those explanations short and practical.
 
 Good pattern:
 
 - "We are doing this because ..."
 - "This helps us avoid ..."
 - "If we skip this, we usually end up ..."
+
+Bad pattern:
+
+- giving a full polished diagnosis before asking anything
+- silently inventing details and treating them as facts
@@
 ## If the user answers weakly
 
 Do not stall.
 
 Instead:
@@
 Example:
 
 - "Right now I see three likely directions: workflow design, execution habits, or unclear expectations. Which one feels closest, or is there a fourth direction?"
+
+## If the user gives only one sentence
+
+Do not expand it into a full case study by yourself.
+
+Instead:
+
+- classify fit
+- explain the next step
+- give answer directions
+- ask the smallest useful next question
*** End Patch

+# Output Format
+
+Use two formats:
+
+1. turn format for normal interactive mode
+2. final format when enough information exists
+
+## Turn format
+
+Use this in normal interactive execution.
+
+### 1. 适配判断
+
+- 是否适合六问法：
+- 问题类型：
+- 判断理由：
+- 下一步：
+
+If the answer is not fit, stop here after giving the simpler handling path.
+
+### 2. 当前步骤
+
+- 当前在第几步：
+- 这一步为什么要做：
+
+### 3. 暂定理解
+
+- 我目前的理解：
+
+If details are uncertain, clearly say this is provisional.
+
+### 4. 回答方向
+
+Give 2-4 directions when helpful.
+
+Use concrete Chinese.
+
+### 5. 请你回答
+
+Ask one concrete next question.
+
+Then stop and wait.
+
+## Final format
+
+Only use the full final format when:
+
+- the user asked for a draft
+- or enough interaction has happened to ground the result
+
+### 1. 适配判断
+
+- 是否适合六问法：
+- 问题类型：
+- 判断理由：
+- 下一步：
+
+### 2. 原始问题
+
+- 原始表述：
+
+### 3. 支撑层
+
+- 可能领域：
+- 使用框架：
+- 为什么用这个框架：
+
+### 4. 轮次过程
+
+For each round, include:
+
+#### 第 N 轮
+
+- 第1问 目标是什么：
+- 第2问 现状是什么：
+- 第3问 差距是什么：
+- 第4问 目标背后的目的是什么：
+- 第4问 0.5Why 验证：

+- 第6问 差距真正的原因是什么：
+
+#### 是否重开
+
+- 是否需要重开：
+- 如果需要，为什么：
+- 新问题是什么：
+
+### 5. 最终结果
+
+- 真正的问题：
+- 限制因素：
+- 可改变因素：
+- 初步行动方向：
+
+### 6. 教练说明
+
+Keep this short.
+
+Include:
+
+- 为什么这次关键判断重要
+- 用户下次可以复用什么
+- 还有什么不确定
+
+## Style notes
+
+- 默认中文
+- 默认互动
+- 不要在信息很少时直接写出完整终局分析
+- 不要把暂定推断写成既定事实
*** End Patch

@@
 # Six-Questions Workflow
 
 ## Core sequence
@@
 7. Restate the real problem
 
 Do not skip steps.
+
+## Interaction rule
+
+Default mode is not one-shot completion.
+
+For each unresolved step:
+
+1. explain the step briefly
+2. state the temporary understanding
+3. give answer directions if needed
+4. ask one focused question
+5. wait for the user's reply
+
+Only continue without waiting if:
+
+- the user explicitly asks for a draft
+- or the current step is already grounded by the user's earlier information and only needs a short confirmation
@@
 ## Question 1. What is the goal
@@
 Useful prompts:
 
 - What outcome do you want?
 - By when?
 - What would count as solved?
 - Can we make that more concrete or measurable?
+
+Chinese default prompts:
+
+- 你真正想达到的结果是什么？
+- 你希望在什么时间内做到？
+- 到什么程度才算解决？
+- 我先给你几个方向，你来选最接近的一个，可以吗？
@@
 Useful prompts:
 
 - What is happening now?
 - What is the current level, count, rate, or condition?
 - If nothing changes, what will happen by the target time?
+
+Chinese default prompts:
+
+- 现在真实发生的情况是什么？
+- 如果什么都不改，到目标时间点大概会怎样？
+- 你可以从频率、结果、行为表现里任选一个角度先回答。
@@
 If question 3 shows the original problem is not a valid problem:
@@
 3. restart the workflow from question 1
+
+Chinese default prompts:
+
+- 现在看，原始问题真的成立吗？
+- 真正的差距更像落在什么地方？
+- 如果原问题不成立，我们要不要换成一个更准确的新问题？
@@
 If question 4 reveals a new real goal:
@@
 4. restart the workflow from question 1 using the new goal
+
+Chinese default prompts:
+
+- 你想要这个目标，背后真正想解决的是什么？
+- 这个目标一定是唯一办法吗？
+- 如果不是唯一办法，那我们真正要解决的问题是哪一个？
@@
 Recommended sub-sequence:
@@
 5. keep digging only on causes that pass
+
+Chinese default prompts:
+
+- 造成当前现状的原因，你觉得更像是结构问题、顺序问题，还是优先级问题？
+- 如果你一时说不全，我先给你几个方向，你来判断哪个最接近。
+- 这个原因真的会直接导致当前现状吗，还是只是背景条件？
*** End Patch

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: D:\code\0324_2025\docs\design\yc_think_v0_1升级分析.md
+# yc_think v0.1 升级分析
+
+## 1. 升级背景
+
+这次升级来自第一次真实测试反馈。
+
+测试 prompt：
+
+- Use yc_think. 我总是拖延本月学习任务，但我也说不清真正的问题是什么。请按这个方法帮我分析。
+
+用户反馈的两个关键问题：
+
+1. 最严重的问题是，模型自己一路回答完了，没有和用户互动走完整个流程。
+2. 每一问最好默认使用中文。
+
+## 2. v0.0 的主要问题
+
+### 问题 1：默认行为太偏“自动完成”
+
+v0.0 的设计虽然强调了六问法结构，但没有把“互动”写成硬约束。
+
+结果是模型会自然选择：
+
+- 用已有信息自行推断
+- 尽快补全整个分析链
+- 直接给出完整结论
+
+这在一般分析任务里看起来像“主动”，但对 `yc_think` 这种教练型 skill 来说是错误行为。
+
+### 问题 2：输出模板默认鼓励“终局答案”
+
+v0.0 的输出模板重点是完整结果：
+
+- 原始问题
+- 支撑层
+- 轮次过程
+- 最终结果
+
+这会让模型倾向于一次把这些都填满，而不是分步互动。
+
+### 问题 3：没有明确区分“互动模式”和“草案模式”
+
+v0.0 没有告诉模型：
+
+- 什么时候该一步步问
+- 什么时候才允许先给一版推演草案
+
+于是模型默认走了“草案模式”，但又没有明确标注这是草案。
+
+### 问题 4：中文要求不够硬
+
+虽然项目上下文是中文，但 skill 内没有把“默认中文”写成强规则。
+
+结果是：
+
+- 大部分内容可能是中文
+- 但步骤名、问题名、部分术语容易偏英文
+
+这会影响体验的一致性。
+
+## 3. v0.1 的设计目标
+
+v0.1 的目标很明确：
+
+1. 把“互动式教练”变成默认执行方式
+2. 把“默认中文”变成明确规则
+3. 把“整段自答”限制为特殊模式
+4. 让较普通的 LLM 也能稳定照着执行
+
+## 4. v0.1 的核心改动
+
+### 改动 1：把互动写成硬规则
+
+在 `SKILL.md` 中新增了这些明确规则：
+
+- 默认不允许一口气完成全部流程
+- 默认一轮只解决一个未完成阶段
+- 每一步都要“解释 -> 给方向 -> 提问 -> 等用户”
+- 只有用户明确要求“先给草案”时，才允许一版推演
+
+### 改动 2：区分互动模式和草案模式
+
+新增两种模式：
+
+#### 互动模式
+
+- 默认模式
+- 一步一步推进
+- 每次只推进当前步骤
+
+#### 草案模式
+
+- 只有用户明确要求时才启用
+- 必须标明“这是暂定草案”
+- 必须标出假设
+
+### 改动 3：输出格式拆成“回合格式”和“最终格式”
+
+v0.1 不再默认让模型直接输出完整终局结构。
+
+现在拆成：
+
+- 回合格式
+  - 适配判断
+  - 当前步骤
+  - 暂定理解
+  - 回答方向
+  - 请你回答
+- 最终格式
+  - 在信息足够、或用户要求草案时才使用
+
+### 改动 4：默认中文
+
+v0.1 明确要求：
+
+- 默认中文输出
+- 默认中文步骤名
+- 默认中文提问
+
+### 改动 5：把“不要自己扩写一整套案例”写清楚
+
+在教练指南中，新增了明确限制：
+
+- 当用户只给一句话时，不要自己扩写成完整案例
+- 先分类、解释、给方向、再问下一步
+
+## 5. 为什么这次改动更适合 yc_think
+
+`yc_think` 的本质不是“自动生成一份六问法报告”，而是：
+
+- 陪用户界定问题
+- 训练用户思维
+- 降低用户回答门槛
+
+如果模型直接一路写完：
+
+- 用户没有参与
+- 用户没有被训练
+- 用户也无法及时纠正模型的假设
+
+所以，互动不是附加体验，而是核心能力。
+
+## 6. v0.1 重点要验证什么
+
+下一轮测试时，优先看下面几点：
+
+### 6.1 互动性
+
+- 是否默认进入互动模式
+- 是否只推进当前一步
+- 是否会停下来等待用户回答
+
+### 6.2 中文一致性
+
+- 步骤名是否中文
+- 提问是否中文
+- 解释是否中文
+
+### 6.3 回答方向是否有帮助
+
+- 是否主动给 2-4 个回答方向
+- 是否足够具体
+- 是否真的降低了用户回答难度
+
+### 6.4 草案模式是否被正确限制
+
+- 普通情况下是否避免直接整段推演
+- 用户明确要求草案时，是否能正确切换
+
+## 7. 版本保留方式
+
+为了保留历史版本：
+
+- `versions/skills/yc-think-0-0` 保留 v0.0
+- `skills/yc-think` 作为当前活跃版本 v0.1
+
+## 8. 当前结论
+
+这次升级不是小修小补，而是把 skill 的默认行为从：
+
+- “自己补完”
+
+改成：
+
+- “先互动，再推进；必要时才草案”
+
+这才更符合 `yc_think` 作为教练型元 skill 的定位。
*** End Patch

*** Add File: D:\code\0324_2025\evals\yc_think_0_1_manual_test_cases.md
+# yc_think v0.1 手工测试用例
+
+## 这版重点看什么
+
+和上一版不同，这一版优先测试：
+
+1. 是否默认互动
+2. 是否默认中文
+3. 是否会停下来等用户回答
+4. 是否会主动给回答方向
+5. 是否只在明确要求时进入草案模式
+
+## 1. 简单 fit，默认互动
+
+### Prompt
+
+Use yc_think. 我总是拖延本月学习任务，但我也说不清真正的问题是什么。请按这个方法帮我分析。
+
+### 期望
+
+- 判断为适合六问法
+- 用中文输出
+- 不直接把整套六问法都写完
+- 先说明第一步要做什么
+- 给 2-4 个回答方向
+- 提一个具体问题并停下来等用户
+
+## 2. 中等 fit，默认互动
+
+### Prompt
+
+Use yc_think. 我想在两个月内提升团队工作效率，但现在只是感觉流程低效，真正问题是什么我不清楚。请你像教练一样带我梳理。
+
+### 期望
+
+- 判断为适合六问法
+- 使用中文
+- 补团队/流程视角
+- 主动给回答方向
+- 默认分步推进，不直接写完整终局分析
+
+## 3. 复杂 fit，可能触发重开
+
+### Prompt
+
+Use yc_think. 我想这个月离职，因为我觉得在公司没有前途了。你不要直接给建议，先帮我分析这是不是我真正的问题。
+
+### 期望
+
+- 先分类，不急着给建议
+- 能识别这可能是“方案化问题”或“选择问题”
+- 若进入六问法，第 4 问附近应可能重开
+- 仍然保持互动，不直接一口气给完整答案
+
+## 4. 非 fit 信息问题
+
+### Prompt
+
+Use yc_think. SMART 原则是什么意思？
+
+### 期望
+
+- 判断为不适合六问法
+- 说明这是什么问题
+- 直接回答，不硬套六问法
+
+## 5. 非 fit 情绪问题
+
+### Prompt
+
+Use yc_think. 我现在特别烦，什么都不想做。
+
+### 期望
+
+- 先接住情绪
+- 不强推六问法
+- 问用户更想要分析、支持，还是两者都要
+
+## 6. 草案模式测试
+
+### Prompt
+
+Use yc_think. 先别互动，直接按现有信息给我一版初步分析，后面我再修正。
+
+### 期望
+
+- 明确说明这是“初步草案”
+- 标明假设
+- 使用六问法结构
+- 最后邀请用户修正
*** End Patch

Output:
# yc_think v0.1 升级分析

## 1. 升级背景

这次升级来自第一次真实测试反馈。

测试 prompt：

- Use yc_think. 我总是拖延本月学习任务，但我也说不清真正的问题是什么。请按这个方法帮我分析。

用户反馈的两个关键问题：

1. 最严重的问题是，模型自己一路回答完了，没有和用户互动走完整个流程。
2. 每一问最好默认使用中文。

## 2. v0.0 的主要问题

### 问题 1：默认行为太偏“自动完成”

v0.0 的设计虽然强调了六问法结构，但没有把“互动”写成硬约束。

结果是模型会自然选择：

- 用已有信息自行推断
- 尽快补全整个分析链
- 直接给出完整结论

这在一般分析任务里看起来像“主动”，但对 `yc_think` 这种教练型 skill 来说是错误行为。

### 问题 2：输出模板默认鼓励“终局答案”

v0.0 的输出模板重点是完整结果：

- 原始问题
- 支撑层
- 轮次过程
- 最终结果

这会让模型倾向于一次把这些都填满，而不是分步互动。

### 问题 3：没有明确区分“互动模式”和“草案模式”

v0.0 没有告诉模型：

- 什么时候该一步步问
- 什么时候才允许先给一版推演草案

于是模型默认走了“草案模式”，但又没有明确标注这是草案。

### 问题 4：中文要求不够硬

虽然项目上下文是中文，但 skill 内没有把“默认中文”写成强规则。

结果是：

- 大部分内容可能是中文
- 但步骤名、问题名、部分术语容易偏英文

这会影响体验的一致性。

## 3. v0.1 的设计目标

v0.1 的目标很明确：

1. 把“互动式教练”变成默认执行方式
2. 把“默认中文”变成明确规则
3. 把“整段自答”限制为特殊模式
4. 让较普通的 LLM 也能稳定照着执行

## 4. v0.1 的核心改动

### 改动 1：把互动写成硬规则

在 `SKILL.md` 中新增了这些明确规则：

- 默认不允许一口气完成全部流程
- 默认一轮只解决一个未完成阶段
- 每一步都要“解释 -> 给方向 -> 提问 -> 等用户”
- 只有用户明确要求“先给草案”时，才允许一版推演

### 改动 2：区分互动模式和草案模式

新增两种模式：

#### 互动模式

- 默认模式
- 一步一步推进
- 每次只推进当前步骤

#### 草案模式

- 只有用户明确要求时才启用
- 必须标明“这是暂定草案”
- 必须标出假设

### 改动 3：输出格式拆成“回合格式”和“最终格式”

v0.1 不再默认让模型直接输出完整终局结构。

现在拆成：

- 回合格式
  - 适配判断
  - 当前步骤
  - 暂定理解
  - 回答方向
  - 请你回答
- 最终格式
  - 在信息足够、或用户要求草案时才使用

### 改动 4：默认中文

v0.1 明确要求：

- 默认中文输出
- 默认中文步骤名
- 默认中文提问

### 改动 5：把“不要自己扩写一整套案例”写清楚

在教练指南中，新增了明确限制：

- 当用户只给一句话时，不要自己扩写成完整案例
- 先分类、解释、给方向、再问下一步

## 5. 为什么这次改动更适合 yc_think

`yc_think` 的本质不是“自动生成一份六问法报告”，而是：

- 陪用户界定问题
- 训练用户思维
- 降低用户回答门槛

如果模型直接一路写完：

- 用户没有参与
- 用户没有被训练
- 用户也无法及时纠正模型的假设

所以，互动不是附加体验，而是核心能力。

## 6. v0.1 重点要验证什么

下一轮测试时，优先看下面几点：

### 6.1 互动性

- 是否默认进入互动模式
- 是否只推进当前一步
- 是否会停下来等待用户回答

### 6.2 中文一致性

- 步骤名是否中文
- 提问是否中文
- 解释是否中文

### 6.3 回答方向是否有帮助

- 是否主动给 2-4 个回答方向
- 是否足够具体
- 是否真的降低了用户回答难度

### 6.4 草案模式是否被正确限制

- 普通情况下是否避免直接整段推演
- 用户明确要求草案时，是否能正确切换

## 7. 版本保留方式

为了保留历史版本：

- `versions/skills/yc-think-0-0` 保留 v0.0
- `skills/yc-think` 作为当前活跃版本 v0.1

## 8. 当前结论

这次升级不是小修小补，而是把 skill 的默认行为从：

- “自己补完”

改成：

- “先互动，再推进；必要时才草案”

这才更符合 `yc_think` 作为教练型元 skill 的定位。

我已经保留了旧版本，并做了新的活跃版本：

- 旧版归档：[versions\skills\yc-think-0-0](D:\code\0324_2025\versions\skills\yc-think-0-0)
- 新版活跃版：[skills\yc-think](D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think)

这次新版的重点修正就两个，但都是硬规则，不再是建议：

- 默认必须互动，不能自己把整套流程答完
- 默认必须中文，步骤名、提问、解释都用中文

具体改动我整理在这里：
- 升级分析：[yc_think_v0_1升级分析.md](D:\code\0324_2025\docs\design\yc_think_v0_1升级分析.md)

- 新版教练规则：[coaching-guidelines.md](D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think\references\coaching-guidelines.md)
- 新版流程规则：[six-questions-workflow.md](D:\code\0324_2025\skills\yc-think\references\six-questions-workflow.md)

这版你再用同一个 prompt 测，**正确的第一轮行为**应该更像这样，而不是直接给完整诊断：
